summary:
The Superior Olive within the Superior Olivary Complex controls the stapedial reflex as it supplies the motor innervation via the nervus intermedius nucleus that reaches the stapedius muscle. This pathway ensures proper regulation of middle ear movement during loud sounds.
```

The stapedial reflex is controlled by the Superior Olivary Complex due to its direct involvement in providing motor innervation to the stapedius muscle via the facial nerve (VII). ```
